name: Tyler Conklin
headline: Veteran tight end fades into a Detroit backup role
body:
Conklins 2025 was a sharp fall from relevance: signed to the Chargers, he played 6 games on a 19.2 percent snap share, ran just 79 routes, and caught 7 of 10 targets for 101 yards with no touchdowns and a -0.368 EPA per play, ranking TE70. That collapse followed two years as a dependable Jets volume target: 51 catches, 449 yards, 4 scores, and 70.9 points (TE27) in 2024, and 61 catches, 621 yards, and an 80-plus percent route share in 2023 (TE13).

Now in Detroit behind Sam LaPorta, the 30-year-old is a clear backup with no path to the target volume that powered his peak (15.5 percent share in 2023 versus 1.8 percent in 2025). His value depends entirely on a LaPorta injury, and even then the Lions pass-game pecking order limits the ceiling. There is no standalone redraft case here. He is a waiver-wire name to file away as an injury contingency, nothing more.
